title: RJ45 Connector & Wiring Standards
description: The 8-pin modular connector used for Ethernet — defines pin assignments for T568A and T568B wiring.
fullExplanation: RJ45 (Registered Jack 45) is the standard connector for Ethernet cables. It has 8 pins (positions) that connect to the 4 twisted pairs in UTP cable. Two wiring standards exist: T568A and T568B — they differ only in which pairs connect to pins 1/2 and 3/6. T568B is more common in North America. A straight-through cable uses the same standard on both ends. A crossover cable uses T568A on one end and T568B on the other (needed to connect like devices — now obsolete due to Auto-MDIX).
keyFacts: T568B is the most common wiring standard in North America
T568A is required by US government (TIA-568) and common in Europe
Straight-through: same standard both ends — connects different device types (PC to switch)
Crossover: T568A one end, T568B other — connects same device types (PC to PC)
Auto-MDIX: modern switches/NICs auto-detect and swap TX/RX — crossover cables obsolete
Gigabit Ethernet uses all 4 pairs bidirectionally (1000BASE-T)
PoE uses spare pairs (4,5 and 7,8) or data pairs for power delivery
